summary: Close the remaining viewer elapsed-time identity and N-4 unit-localization findings by resetting time for every engine job and routing user-visible time, distance, and dimension units through the message catalog.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.topic: Render job identity and localized units
decisions.decision: Thread the active engine job ID from Workbench through ViewerPaneConnector to ViewerPane and make it part of the elapsed-timer effect identity. Add catalog functions for compact console seconds, millimeter values, and millimeter dimensions, and require each previously hard-coded product surface to call them.
decisions.rationale: Rendering status remains `rendering` across supersession, so status alone cannot identify the clock origin and visibly overstates the replacement job duration. User-visible units are language content even when the current English rendering stays byte-for-byte identical; centralizing them in the message catalog satisfies N-4 and preserves existing acceptance copy.

testEvidence.criterion: A superseding render starts a new visible elapsed clock
testEvidence.test: Keep ViewerPane continuously in rendering status, advance job A to 1.2 seconds, rerender with job B, and require an immediate 0.0-second display followed by 0.2 seconds after the new interval advances.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPane.test.tsx tests/ui/message-catalog-units.test.ts --reporter=verbose
red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: the superseding job still displayed `Rendering… 1.2 s` where `Rendering… 0.0 s` was required. The initial catalog-policy test path was also one directory too high and was corrected before implementation evidence was accepted.

green.command:
pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPane.test.tsx tests/ui/message-catalog-units.test.ts tests/ui/diagnostics/DiagnosticConsole.test.tsx tests/ui/files/ProjectExportDialog.test.tsx tests/ui/viewer/ViewerDetailsPanel.test.tsx --reporter=verbose
pnpm.cmd typecheck
green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 5 passed (5); Tests 23 passed (23); Duration 1.76s. The superseding job reset to 0.0 seconds and advanced independently to 0.2 seconds. TypeScript no-emit exited 0.

testEvidence.criterion: User-visible elapsed, distance, and dimension units use message-catalog authority
testEvidence.test: Inspect the five affected product surfaces and require calls to the dedicated message-catalog functions, then run their component behavior tests to preserve existing visible English output.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/message-catalog-units.test.ts --reporter=verbose (isolated test-only worktree at baseline commit 3e41b42)
red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 1 failed (1). The first required product source, DiagnosticConsole.tsx, did not contain `messages.consoleElapsedSeconds(`. The initial combined run had exposed a one-directory test-path setup mistake; the path was corrected and this isolated baseline replay supplied the valid behavior-level falsification without altering the implementation worktree.

green.command:
pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPane.test.tsx tests/ui/message-catalog-units.test.ts tests/ui/diagnostics/DiagnosticConsole.test.tsx tests/ui/files/ProjectExportDialog.test.tsx tests/ui/viewer/ViewerDetailsPanel.test.tsx --reporter=verbose
pnpm.cmd typecheck
green.outcome: GREEN: the source-policy assertion and all affected component suites passed within 5 files / 23 tests; existing `+0.003s`, millimeter distances, bounds, and export summary copy remained unchanged. TypeScript no-emit exited 0.

questions: No owner similarity gate, comparison repository, similarity workflow content, M3 work, installer work, or prohibited source was opened, executed, or modified.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
